senses.definition: compelled forcibly by an outside agency
examples: mobs goaded by blind hatred

senses.definition: urged or forced to action through moral pressure
examples: felt impelled to take a stand against the issue

senses.definition: strongly motivated to succeed
operate or control a vehicle
examples: drive a car or bus
Can you drive this four-wheel truck?

senses.definition: travel or be transported in a vehicle
examples: We drove to the university every morning
They motored to London for the theater

senses.definition: cause someone or something to move by driving
examples: She drove me to school every day
We drove the car to the garage

senses.definition: force into or from an action or state, either physically or metaphorically
examples: She rammed her mind into focus
He drives me mad

senses.definition: to compel or force or urge relentlessly or exert coercive pressure on, or motivate strongly
examples: She is driven by her passion

senses.definition: cause to move back by force or influence
examples: repel the enemy
push back the urge to smoke
beat back the invaders

senses.definition: compel somebody to do something, often against their own will or judgment
examples: She finally drove him to change jobs

senses.definition: push, propel, or press with force
examples: Drive a nail into the wall

senses.definition: cause to move rapidly by striking or throwing with force
examples: drive the ball far out into the field

senses.definition: strive and make an effort to reach a goal
examples: She tugged for years to make a decent living
We have to push a little to make the deadline!
She is driving away at her doctoral thesis

senses.definition: move into a desired direction of discourse
examples: What are you driving at?

senses.definition: have certain properties when driven
examples: This car rides smoothly
My new truck drives well

senses.definition: work as a driver
examples: He drives a bread truck
She drives for the taxi company in Newark

senses.definition: move by being propelled by a force
examples: The car drove around the corner

senses.definition: urge forward
examples: drive the cows into the barn

senses.definition: proceed along in a vehicle
examples: We drive the turnpike to work

senses.definition: strike with a driver, as in teeing off
examples: drive a golf ball

senses.definition: hit very hard, as by swinging a bat horizontally
examples: drive a ball

senses.definition: excavate horizontally
examples: drive a tunnel

senses.definition: cause to function by supplying the force or power for or by controlling
examples: The amplifier drives the tube
steam drives the engines
this device drives the disks for the computer

senses.definition: (hunting) search for game
examples: drive the forest

senses.definition: (hunting) chase from cover into more open ground
examples: drive the game
